MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Miljoy B. Linsao of McNAMARA, NEY, BEATTY & SLATTERY (925-939-5330), appearing for defendant / moving party Brand Energy Services of California, Inc.
Anne T. Acuna of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Patrick Farley

RULING:
DEFENDANT BRAND ENERGY SERVICES OF CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Brand Energy Services of California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and premises owner/contractor liability and taken off calendar as to strict liability. Defendant did not satisfy its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's failure to demonstrate with its moving papers that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claims against defendant. Assuming without deciding that the declarations of Mr. Singletary, Mr. Valentine and Mr. Kuhn are admissible, when read in conjunction with plaintiff's deposition testimony they merely create a triable issue whether defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame. Those declarations do not sustain defendant's burden of demonstrating that defendant never performed work at Shell Oil that involved the disturbance of asbestos-containing materials. Plaintiff represents that he has dismissed his strict liability cause of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Miriam P. Maxwell of VASQUEZ ESTRADA & CONWAY LLP (415-453-0555), appearing for defendant / moving party Monterey Mechanical
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Patrick Farley

RULING:
DEFENDANT CHERNE CONTRACTING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Cherne Contracting Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and special interrogatory responses preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. Assuming without deciding that the declarations submitted with defendant's moving papers of Ms. Abrahamson and Mr. Koenen are admissible, when read in conjunction with plaintiff's deposition testimony they merely create a triable issue whether defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame. Defendant's moving papers did not make an issue of anything other than whether defendant worked at Shell Oil in Martinez during the relevant time frame and thus that was the only issue plaintiff had to respond to. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Carol Haraburda of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 8052 (503/TLJ)
